Vuyargur is facing a dire tool shortage.

※Please note that the difficulty of this quest has been synced to your current level. Furthermore, you may not proceed with a class or job that is different from when you accepted this quest.

1: Speak with the masonry supervisor at Zirgorteh the Open-armed.

2: Deliver the requested items to the masonry supervisor.

24: Speak with the masonry supervisor at Zirgorteh the Open-armed.

48: Ah, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. Just the <If(PlayerParameter(4))>woman<Else/>man</If> I wanted to see. We have a shortage of tools at Masons' Work, and I was hoping you could help shore up our supplies.

49: This is rather an urgent task, as I would rather not have everyone idle for too long. Go speak with the supervisor at Masons' Work to find out exactly what we need.

50: You're the one helping us with the new tools? I'm glad to have you─we didn't expect so many eager hands to join us, and now we have to make them productive. So let's get right to it.

51: We need hammers, and lots of them. Luckily, there should be more than enough materials in this crate.

52: We need measuring tools, and lots of them. Luckily, there should be more than enough materials in this crate.

53: We need polish for the stone, and lots of it. Luckily, there should be more than enough materials in this crate.

54: The crate appears to be filled to the brim with sundry materials─none of which you require at this time.

57: You obtain additional crafting materials from the crate.

60: Finished yet? I hope so─our workers are getting restless without a task to keep them busy.

61: Ah, these will do. These will do splendidly! I'll pass these around straightaway.

62: With the right tools in the right hands, everything seems to be back on schedule.

63: This is among the largest projects I have ever helped oversee, and there simply are not enough experienced stonemasons to meet our demand. So we have begun teaching the new volunteers.

64: Such skills are not learned overnight, as you well know, and every mason worth his salt has his fair share of worn-down tools to thank for his mastery.

65: What you made us today will be put through its paces, you can be sure. If we need more─and I have a feeling we will─I hope we can count on you again.
